notes: MAINSTREET is the public square — Frasier reads, sometimes posts, the enemy reads too. Sprint 2 fills it out to 8 threads spread across the summer.

op.body:
The summer's started. The river is up two inches from where it was last Tuesday. The cathedral's AC unit gave out Wednesday — replacement parts are in the mail. The storefront's Friday menu is on at 6 — Hector's new andouille dish is on it.

Reports come in on the usual schedule.

  — F.

replies.body: Picked up the new loom warp Friday. Phase three lot is the phase three lot. — Mac
Counter's open through the holiday. Faith II is under it. Coffee's hot.

threads.subject: someone asked me about the cypresses again

op.body:
Forwarded query from a regular asking where the private cypress thread is now that we've cleaned up the old links. The thread moved last summer — it's on the dial that ends in 5550106. Anyone with the standing can dial.

  — F.

replies.body: Confirmed. Same masthead. New sysop took it over in March.

op.body:
Anyone documenting the storms from the south parish this week? I have B-roll from the bungalow but the levee shots are off my route. If you have anything I can include in the rough cut by Sunday, my DM is open.

— E.

replies.body: I'll forward what I have. The county camera went out Tuesday afternoon — the footage post-Tuesday is going to be photos and what I can pull off the volunteer-monitor traffic. I'll mail it tonight.

threads.subject: phone outage at the bungalow · still down

op.body:
Bungalow line still down three days in. Bell says Friday earliest. If anyone needs to reach me try the storefront — T. will take a message and I'll pick up at 6.

— E.

replies.body: Confirmed. The message pad's behind the register. Faith II runs it. — T.
Bell's been pulling the south-parish lines for the new junction box. They never warn the people on the line. I'll come look at the bungalow's panel Thursday if it's still dead.

op.body:
The cathedral's closing at noon Thursday for the holiday. The storefront's open through dinner; T. is running the kitchen alone since Hector's taking the day with his boy. The boathouse is closed all weekend — STEAMBOAT_72's drove down to Mobile for the bell-buoy regatta. River's high enough that the small-craft notice is on through Saturday.

  — F.

replies.body: Regatta's the 6th. I'll be back Sunday night. Don't take the johnboat out — the dock cleat on the left is loose and I haven't gotten to it.

threads.subject: report from the south parish · the wire is up

op.body:
The volunteer monitor line is back up. Calls from south parish should route to the regular number again. Note: the recording on the auto-attendant still has March's date in it; I'll fix it Tuesday. If you hear March, you've reached the right line.

  — F.

threads.subject: the cathedral bell · Sunday schedule changes

op.body:
Effective this Sunday, the 8 AM bell pull moves to 7:45 to align with the parish's new mass time. The 11 AM stays. The Wednesday evening pull at 7 stays.

Please pass the word.

— the aide.

replies.body: Noted. I'll update the bell-pull schedule on the cathedral door this evening.
Same shift happened down here in '92. The 7:45 will feel early for two weeks and then it'll feel normal.

threads.subject: labor day cookout · save the date

op.body:
The storefront's hosting the labor day cookout in the back lot. Sunday the 1st, 4 PM until the brisket runs out. Bring a side; Hector's bringing the brisket and the cornbread and the look of a man who slept three hours.

Mom's bringing the okra.

— T.

replies.body: I'll bring the chairs from the cathedral. The good ones.
